result_summary: Bautista separates TEC, fibroblast, vascular, perivascular, cycling, capsule-associated and minor contaminant compartments. Heimli CD45-depleted libraries recover epithelial, TEC-like, fibroblast, perivascular and endothelial states.
claim_boundary: Downsampled exploratory annotations; not final tissue abundance estimates.

supporting_dataset: Bautista GSE147520 as stromal reference only
result_summary: Heimli contains a MS4A1/CD79A/MHC-II B-cell APC cluster. Bautista does not support strong B-cell claims and should not be used as primary B-cell evidence.
claim_boundary: B-cell APC interpretation should be based on Heimli RNA-only and ADT-supported evidence.

result_summary: Heimli recovers LYZ/MHC-II macrophage-monocyte APC, CCR7/LAMP3 activated DC-like, and LILRA4/IL3RA/GZMB pDC-like clusters.
claim_boundary: Cluster 10 remains low-confidence activated APC-like; final claims should focus on clusters 6, 11 and 12.

result_summary: Bautista shows TEC and MHC-II-associated epithelial/stromal programmes. Heimli recovers cTEC-like and mTEC-like clusters, including antigen-presenting mTEC-like states.
claim_boundary: Separate epithelial MHC-II signal from true immune APC markers.

result_summary: CD45-depleted libraries are enriched for epithelial/stromal/vascular states; unenriched libraries are dominated by thymocyte/T-cell states; APC-enriched libraries contain immune/APC and mixed ADT-high states.
claim_boundary: Balanced downsampling means composition percentages are exploratory, not absolute abundance.

result_summary: Single-cell labels now define candidate signatures for spatial projection: TEC, cTEC-like, mTEC-like, B-cell APC, macrophage APC, activated DC, pDC, fibroblast, endothelial and perivascular.
claim_boundary: Spatial claims require separate Visium processing and spot-level signature scoring.
